description: A loosely painted scene showing two dark-robed figures in an embrace near a building on the left, while a luminous white-clad figure accompanied by a small dog approaches from the right on a sunlit path. The landscape is pastoral with hills in the background. The painting style is impressionistic with broad, expressive brushstrokes and warm golden light.
theological_description: The Return of Tobias depicts the joyful conclusion of the Book of Tobit, a deuterocanonical narrative. Young Tobias, sent on a journey by his blind father Tobit, was accompanied and protected by the angel Raphael in disguise. Raphael guided Tobias to collect the gall of a fish that would heal his father's blindness. The luminous figure represents either Raphael or the returning Tobias, glowing with divine favor. The story is a profound meditation on God's providential care — even when God seems absent, he sends angels to guide, protect, and heal. The faithful dog is a charming detail preserved from the biblical text.

scripture_refs.reason: Tobias returns home and heals his father Tobit's blindness
Raphael accompanies Tobias on his journey in disguise
He will command his angels concerning you to guard you in all your ways
